summary: iOS 👀 FALCON | DFU - App incorrectly shows device upto date after failure
scenario: App falsely displays "updated successfully" after DFU failure on headset, showing "update unavailable" on retry
description_clean: After a DFU (Device Firmware Update) failure for the headset, the app incorrectly displays the device as up to date. When the user retries the update, the app shows "update unavailable" and later displays "updated successfully" even though the headset was not actually updated.
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android
Navigate to Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth headset is connected
Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Firmware Update
Initiate a Device Firmware Update (DFU) for the headset
Allow the DFU process to fail (e.g., by interrupting Bluetooth connection or powering off the headset during update)
Observe the firmware status displayed on the Firmware Update screen after the failure
Tap 'Retry' or attempt to initiate the update again
Observe the message shown on screen
Wait for the app to display the update completion message
expected: After a DFU failure, the app should correctly report that the firmware update failed and prompt the user to retry. On retry, the app should either allow a fresh update attempt or clearly indicate that the update is unavailable due to the failed state. The app should never falsely report 'updated successfully' when the headset firmware was not actually updated.
actual: After the DFU failure, the app incorrectly displays the device as 'up to date'. When the user retries the update, the app shows 'update unavailable'. Subsequently, the app displays 'updated successfully' even though the headset was not actually updated.
